descriptions.manageCustomAgents: Create and manage custom AI agents

prompts.pasteError: Paste the error from Arduino output window (red line with "error:")
prompts.systemPrompt: You are a helpful Arduino programming assistant. Always respond clearly and explain Arduino code understandably.
prompts.explainCode:
Explain this Arduino code in simple, understandable language:

```cpp
{0}
```

Explain:
- What the code does
- How it works
- Which hardware it controls
- Important concepts for beginners

Be friendly and thorough.
prompts.improveCodeSuffix: Return only the improved code with brief comments on changes.
prompts.addCommentsSelected:
Add helpful comments to this Arduino code:

```cpp
{0}
```

Rules:
- Explain what each important line does
- Comment functions and their parameters
- Explain hardware interactions
- Use // for single-line and /* */ for multi-line comments
- Comments should help beginners
prompts.addComments:
Add helpful comments to this Arduino code:

```cpp
{0}
```

Rules:
- Explain what each important line does
- Comment functions and their parameters
- Explain hardware interactions
- Use // for single-line and /* */ for multi-line comments
- Comments should help beginners
prompts.addCommentsSuffix: Return ONLY the commented code, no explanations around it.
prompts.explainError:
Explain and solve Arduino compiler error:

Error: {0}

Code context (around line {1}):
```cpp
{2}
```

Please explain:
1. What does this error mean?
2. Why does it occur?
3. How do I fix it?
4. Show the corrected code

Explain simply and understandably.
prompts.analyzeSerial:
Analyze this Arduino Serial Monitor output and explain what it means:

Serial Output:
{0}

{1}

Explain:
- What the output means
- Possible problems
- Solution suggestions
prompts.hardwareDebug:
Help with hardware troubleshooting for this Arduino code:

```cpp
{0}
```

Please check:
- Typical wiring errors
- Pin conflicts
- Power supply issues
- Pull-up/pull-down resistors
- Compatibility issues
prompts.addDebugStatements:
Add strategic debug output to this Arduino code:

```cpp
{0}
```

Add Serial.print() statements to:
- Monitor variable values
- Track program flow
- Measure timing
- Find errors
prompts.analyzeTiming:
Analyze timing problems in this Arduino code:

```cpp
{0}
```

Check for:
- Blocking delays
- millis() overflow
- Race conditions
- Interrupt conflicts

Show improved non-blocking code.
prompts.askAI: What would you like to ask the AI?
prompts.askAIGeneral:
User question: {0}

Respond clearly and helpfully.
prompts.addCommentsFile:
Add helpful comments to this complete Arduino file ({0}):

```cpp
{1}
```

Rules:
- Explain what each important line does
- Comment functions and their parameters
- Explain hardware interactions
- Use // for single-line and /* */ for multi-line comments
- Comments should help beginners
prompts.addCommentsSketch:
Add helpful comments to all files in this Arduino sketch:

{0}

Rules:
- Explain what each important line does
- Comment functions and their parameters
- Explain hardware interactions and file relationships
- Use // for single-line and /* */ for multi-line comments
- Comments should help beginners
prompts.explainCodeFile:
Explain this complete Arduino file ({0}):

```cpp
{1}
```

Explain:
- What the file does (main purpose)
- Important functions and their tasks
- Hardware interactions (pins, sensors, etc.)
- Algorithms and logic
- Special features or important details
prompts.explainCodeSketch:
Explain all files in this Arduino sketch:

{0}

Explain:
- What the entire sketch does (main purpose)
- How the files work together
- Important functions in each file
- Hardware interactions (pins, sensors, etc.)
- Data flow between files
- Special features or important details
prompts.explainErrorFile:
Explain this Arduino error in line {1}:

Error: {0}

File ({2}):
```cpp
{3}
```

Explain:
- What the error means
- Why it occurs
- How to fix it
- Show corrected code
prompts.explainErrorSketch:
Explain this Arduino error in line {1}:

Error: {0}

Complete sketch:
{2}

Explain:
- What the error means
- Why it occurs (also in context of other files)
- How to fix it
- Show corrected code
prompts.analyzeSerialFile:
Analyze this serial output:

{0}

Code file ({1}):
```cpp
{2}
```

Find out:
- What the output means
- If there are problems
- What code generates this output
prompts.analyzeSerialSketch:
Analyze this serial output:

{0}

Complete sketch:
{1}

Find out:
- What the output means
- If there are problems
- Which code generates this output
prompts.hardwareDebugFile:
Analyze hardware problems in this file ({0}):

```cpp
{1}
```

Check:
- Pin configurations
- Electrical connections
- Timing issues
- Common hardware errors
prompts.hardwareDebugSketch:
Analyze hardware problems in the complete sketch:

{0}

Check:
- Pin configurations across all files
- Electrical connections
- Timing issues
- Common hardware errors
prompts.addDebugStatementsFile:
Add helpful debug output to this file ({0}):

```cpp
{1}
```

Add Serial.print() statements to:
- Monitor variable values
- Track program flow
- Narrow down error sources
prompts.addDebugStatementsSketch:
Add helpful debug output to this sketch:

{0}

Add Serial.print() statements to:
- Monitor variable values
- Track program flow
- Narrow down error sources
- Understand interactions between files
prompts.analyzeTimingFile:
Analyze timing problems in this file ({0}):

```cpp
{1}
```

Check:
- Use of delay()
- Blocking vs. non-blocking code
- millis() implementation
- Race conditions
prompts.analyzeTimingSketch:
Analyze timing problems in the complete sketch:

{0}

Check:
- Use of delay() across all files
- Blocking vs. non-blocking code
- millis() implementation
- Race conditions
- Timing between different files

placeholders.customInstructions: e.g., "don't use millis()", "optimize for Arduino Nano", "add error handling"
placeholders.errorExample: error: 'xc' was not declared in this scope
placeholders.askAI: e.g. "How does PWM work?", "Why isn't my sensor working?"

...
```

...
```

```cpp

...
```

...

...
```

// ========== FileName.ino ==========
...
```

context.noContextDetail: No code files will be shown to the AI
context.selectionDetail: Only the selected code, without additional context
context.currentFileDetailWithSelection: Selected code + rest of file as additional context
context.fullSketchDetailWithSelection: Selected code + all files as additional context
context.fullSketchDetailNoSelection: All files in the project as context
context.minimalContextDetail: Only code around the error line
context.selectLevel: How much context should the AI receive?
context.explanation: The following files are part of the project and help with understanding:
context.focusReminder: IMPORTANT: Focus on the selected code, use the rest as context!

support.message: 🎉 AI.duino has helped you {0} times already!
support.description: This project is free and open source. A small donation helps with further development.

descriptions.apiTimeout: Timeout for API requests in milliseconds
descriptions.autoCheckExtensionUpdates: Check for extension updates on startup
descriptions.maxMessagesPerChat: Maximum messages per chat (older ones will be deleted)
descriptions.inlineCompletionContextLines: Number of lines before cursor as context
descriptions.maxCustomAgents: Maximum number of storable custom agents (1-100)
descriptions.chatHistoryLength: Number of messages in chat history (pairs of user/AI)
descriptions.cardStyle: Visual style for chat and agent selection cards
descriptions.codeTemperature: Temperature for code generation (0 = precise, 1 = creative). Used for code features and in chat code mode.
descriptions.projectNotesEnabled: Enable project notes (NOTES.ino for project-specific AI instructions)
descriptions.autoOpenInChat: Automatically open AI results in chat instead of output panel
descriptions.showCostWarning: Show warning before expensive API requests
descriptions.costWarningThreshold: Threshold in USD for cost warning (e.g., 0.10 = 10 cents)

uninstall.warning: ⚠️ WARNING: This will completely remove AI.duino!
uninstall.willDelete: The following data will be deleted
uninstall.finalWarning: Final warning: Really delete all AI.duino data permanently?
uninstall.manualStep: Please manually disable/remove the extension in Arduino IDE under File → Preferences → Extensions
